scholarships.name: National Scholarship Portal (NSP) - Post Matric Scholarship for SC Students
scholarships.category: SC (Scheduled Caste) students studying in Class 11 and above including UG, PG, Professional courses
scholarships.amount: Maintenance allowance INR 3,500-13,500/year + full tuition fees (for family income below INR 2.5 lakh/year)
scholarships.eligibility: SC category student. Family annual income below INR 2,50,000. Secured 50% or more in previous exam. Must be studying in a recognized institution.
scholarships.deadline: Usually October-November every year. Check NSP portal for exact dates.

scholarships.name: National Scholarship Portal (NSP) - Post Matric Scholarship for ST Students
scholarships.category: ST (Scheduled Tribe) students studying in Class 11 and above including UG, PG, Professional courses
scholarships.amount: Maintenance allowance INR 3,500-13,500/year + full tuition fees + book grant (for family income below INR 2.5 lakh/year). Hostellers get higher maintenance allowance.
scholarships.eligibility: ST category student. Family annual income below INR 2,50,000. Secured 50% or more in previous exam. Must be studying in a recognized institution.
scholarships.deadline: Usually October-November every year. Check NSP portal for exact dates.

scholarships.name: National Scholarship Portal (NSP) - Post Matric Scholarship for OBC Students
scholarships.category: OBC (Other Backward Classes) students studying in Class 11 and above
scholarships.amount: Maintenance allowance up to INR 1,000/month for hostellers + tuition fees (for family income below INR 1.5 lakh/year)
scholarships.eligibility: OBC (Non-Creamy Layer) student. Family annual income below INR 1,50,000. Must have passed previous exam. Studying in recognized institution.

scholarships.name: Central Sector Scheme of Scholarship for College & University Students
scholarships.category: Meritorious students from families with income below INR 8 lakh/year pursuing UG and PG courses
scholarships.amount: INR 12,000/year for UG (first 3 years), INR 20,000/year for PG. Total: up to INR 56,000 over UG+PG. Increased amount for professional courses.
scholarships.eligibility: Must be in top 20% of 12th board results. Family annual income below INR 8,00,000. Not receiving any other scholarship. Admitted to recognized UG/PG college.

scholarships.name: Prime Minister's Scholarship Scheme (PMSS) for Central Armed Police Forces & Assam Rifles
scholarships.category: Children/widows of CAPF & Assam Rifles personnel (martyred/disabled/ex-servicemen) pursuing professional degree courses
scholarships.amount: INR 3,000/month for boys, INR 3,600/month for girls (for professional UG courses like MBBS, B.Tech, BBA, BCA, B.Pharm, Nursing etc.)
scholarships.eligibility: Ward/widow of CAPF/AR personnel. Minimum 60% in 12th or graduation (for PG courses). Pursuing professional degree from recognized institution. Age below 25 years.
scholarships.apply_at: https://scholarships.gov.in (National Scholarship Portal) or KSB (Kendriya Sainik Board) portal

scholarships.name: Uttarakhand State Merit Scholarship
scholarships.category: Meritorious students of Uttarakhand domicile studying in Class 11 onwards and higher education
scholarships.amount: INR 6,000-12,000/year depending on course level (UG/PG). Additional hostel allowance for students from hill districts.
scholarships.eligibility: Uttarakhand domicile. Passed 10th/12th with 80%+ marks from Uttarakhand Board or CBSE. Family income below INR 3,60,000/year. Admitted to recognized institution.
scholarships.apply_at: https://scholarships.uk.gov.in (Uttarakhand Scholarship Portal) or through college
scholarships.deadline: Varies by year. Usually September-November. Check state portal.

scholarships.name: Uttarakhand SC/ST/OBC Scholarship
scholarships.category: SC/ST/OBC students of Uttarakhand domicile pursuing education from Class 11 to Post-Graduation
scholarships.amount: Full tuition fee reimbursement + maintenance allowance INR 2,000-5,000/month for hostellers. Book and stationery grant included.
scholarships.eligibility: Uttarakhand domicile with SC/ST/OBC certificate. Family income criteria varies by category (below INR 2-2.5 lakh/year). Must be pursuing regular full-time course.
scholarships.apply_at: https://scholarships.uk.gov.in or Social Welfare Department, Uttarakhand
scholarships.deadline: Varies by year. Apply as soon as admission is confirmed.

scholarships.name: Uttarakhand Chief Minister's Medhavi Chhatra Protsahan Yojana
scholarships.category: Brilliant students from economically weaker families in Uttarakhand pursuing higher education
scholarships.amount: INR 12,000-50,000/year depending on course (higher for professional courses like MBBS, B.Tech, MBA). Covers tuition and books.
scholarships.eligibility: Uttarakhand domicile. Scored 80%+ in 12th board exam. Family income below INR 4,80,000/year. Must be admitted to a recognized UG/PG program. No arrears/backlogs.
scholarships.apply_at: https://scholarships.uk.gov.in or Higher Education Department, Uttarakhand

scholarships.name: INSPIRE Scholarship (Innovation in Science Pursuit for Inspired Research)
scholarships.category: Students pursuing B.Sc / B.S. / Integrated M.Sc in Natural & Basic Sciences who are top 1% in 12th board exams
scholarships.amount: INR 80,000/year for 5 years (INR 60,000 scholarship + INR 20,000 summer research project). Total: up to INR 4,00,000.
scholarships.eligibility: Must be in top 1% of Class 12 board exam OR scored 60%+ in KVPY/JEE Advanced/NEET. Pursuing B.Sc/BS/Integrated M.Sc in Natural Sciences (Physics, Chemistry, Mathematics, Biology, Statistics, Geology, Astrophysics, etc.) at a recognized university.

scholarships.name: AICTE Pragati Scholarship for Girls
scholarships.category: Girl students admitted to AICTE-approved technical courses (B.Tech, B.Pharm, B.Arch, MBA, MCA etc.)
scholarships.amount: INR 50,000/year for up to 4 years (covers tuition fees, books, laptops, etc.)
scholarships.eligibility: Girl student admitted to AICTE-approved 1st year technical course. Family income below INR 8,00,000/year. Only 2 girl children per family can avail. Must not be receiving any other scholarship.
scholarships.apply_at: https://scholarships.gov.in (National Scholarship Portal — under AICTE schemes)

scholarships.name: AICTE Saksham Scholarship for Differently Abled Students
scholarships.category: Differently abled students admitted to AICTE-approved technical degree/diploma programs
scholarships.amount: INR 50,000/year for up to 4 years
scholarships.eligibility: Differently abled student (minimum 40% disability) admitted to AICTE-approved degree/diploma program. Family income below INR 8,00,000/year.
scholarships.apply_at: https://scholarships.gov.in (National Scholarship Portal — under AICTE schemes)

scholarships.name: KVPY Fellowship (now merged with INSPIRE)
scholarships.category: Students pursuing basic science courses (B.Sc/BS/Integrated M.Sc) at recognized institutions
scholarships.amount: INR 5,000-7,000/month fellowship + annual contingency grant of INR 20,000-28,000. Valid up to pre-PhD level.
scholarships.eligibility: Now part of INSPIRE SHE scheme. Top performers in 12th board (top 1% in Science stream) pursuing basic sciences. Earlier KVPY exam is discontinued — eligibility now through board exam marks.
scholarships.deadline: Check INSPIRE portal for current deadlines.

scholarships.name: Aam Aadmi Bima Yojana (AABY) Scholarship
scholarships.category: Children of rural landless households studying in Class 9-12
scholarships.amount: INR 1,200/year for Class 9-12 students (2 children per family)
scholarships.eligibility: Parent must be a member of AABY (below poverty line, rural landless household). Student in Class 9-12 in recognized school.
scholarships.apply_at: Through LIC office or State Nodal Agency. Also available on NSP.
scholarships.deadline: Rolling basis. Apply through LIC district office.

scholarships.name: Begum Hazrat Mahal National Scholarship for Minority Girls
scholarships.category: Muslim, Christian, Sikh, Buddhist, Jain, Parsi girl students studying in Class 9-12
scholarships.amount: INR 5,000/year for Class 9-10, INR 6,000/year for Class 11-12
scholarships.eligibility: Girl student from notified minority community. Studying in Class 9-12 in government or recognized school. Scored 50%+ in previous exam. Family annual income below INR 2,00,000.
scholarships.apply_at: https://scholarships.gov.in (National Scholarship Portal) or Maulana Azad Education Foundation website

scholarships.name: Pre-Matric Scholarship for Minority Students
scholarships.category: Minority community students (Muslim, Christian, Sikh, Buddhist, Jain, Parsi) studying in Class 1-10
scholarships.amount: INR 1,000-10,700/year (Class 1-5: INR 1,000/year, Class 6-10: maintenance + admission fee + tuition fee)
scholarships.eligibility: Student from notified minority community. Family income below INR 1,00,000/year. Minimum 50% marks in previous exam. Must not be receiving any other scholarship.

scholarships.name: Post-Matric Scholarship for Minority Students
scholarships.category: Minority community students studying in Class 11 to PhD level
scholarships.amount: Maintenance allowance INR 5,500-13,500/year + full tuition/course fee (up to INR 3,00,000 for professional courses)
scholarships.eligibility: Student from notified minority community. Family income below INR 2,00,000/year. Secured 50%+ in previous exam. Studying in recognized institution in India.

scholarships.name: NMMS (National Means-cum-Merit Scholarship)
scholarships.category: Economically weaker students studying in Class 9 in government schools to continue education up to Class 12
scholarships.amount: INR 12,000/year (INR 1,000/month) for 4 years (Class 9 to 12)
scholarships.eligibility: Student studying in Class 8 in government/government-aided/local body school. Scored 55%+ in Class 7 (50% for SC/ST). Family income below INR 3,50,000/year. Must clear NMMS selection exam.
scholarships.apply_at: https://scholarships.gov.in or through state education department (SCERT Uttarakhand)
scholarships.deadline: Exam usually conducted in November. Registration through school in August-September.

scholarships.name: Reliance Foundation Scholarship
scholarships.category: UG students in STEM (Science, Technology, Engineering, Mathematics), Humanities, and Arts at recognized Indian universities
scholarships.amount: Up to INR 2,00,000/year covering tuition fees, hostel, books, and living expenses. Also includes mentoring and career guidance.
scholarships.eligibility: Indian citizen. Annual family income below INR 15,00,000. Must be admitted to/studying in 1st or 2nd year of UG program at a recognized university. Based on merit + financial need.

scholarships.name: Tata Trusts Scholarship
scholarships.category: Economically disadvantaged students pursuing professional UG and PG courses across India
scholarships.amount: Up to INR 50,000-1,50,000/year depending on course and institution. Covers tuition, books, and maintenance.
scholarships.eligibility: Indian student pursuing professional degree (Engineering, Medical, Law, Management, etc.). Family income below INR 2,50,000/year. Must have secured admission to a recognized institution.
scholarships.deadline: Rolling basis through the year. Apply after securing admission.

scholarships.name: Sitaram Jindal Foundation Scholarship
scholarships.category: Meritorious students from low-income families pursuing education from Class 1 to Post-Graduation and Professional courses
scholarships.amount: INR 1,500-30,000/year depending on course level (higher for medical, engineering, MBA)
scholarships.eligibility: Indian student. Family income below INR 2,00,000/year. Secured 60%+ in previous exam. Pursuing regular full-time course.

scholarships.name: LIC Golden Jubilee Scholarship
scholarships.category: Students from economically weaker sections who have passed 10th/12th and pursuing higher studies
scholarships.amount: INR 20,000/year for students pursuing professional courses (Engineering, Medical, etc.). INR 10,000/year for other courses.
scholarships.eligibility: Family income below INR 1,50,000/year (INR 2,50,000 for special category students). Scored 60%+ in 10th/12th. Admitted to recognized institution.

scholarships.name: Kishore Vaigyanik Protsahan Yojana (KVPY) — Now part of INSPIRE
scholarships.category: Students with aptitude for scientific research pursuing B.Sc/BS/Integrated M.Sc
scholarships.amount: INR 5,000-7,000/month during UG + annual contingency grant. Can be continued during M.Sc and PhD with higher amounts.
scholarships.eligibility: Now integrated into INSPIRE SHE scheme. Students in top 1% of 12th board in science stream and enrolled in basic science UG course. Check INSPIRE portal for updated eligibility.
scholarships.deadline: Check INSPIRE portal for annual deadlines.

scholarships.name: AICTE PG Scholarship (GATE/GPAT Qualified)
scholarships.category: GATE/GPAT qualified students admitted to AICTE-approved M.Tech/M.E./M.Pharm programs
scholarships.amount: INR 12,400/month for 24 months (M.Tech/M.E./M.Pharm). Total: up to INR 2,97,600 over 2 years.
scholarships.eligibility: Valid GATE/GPAT score. Admitted to AICTE-approved M.Tech/M.E./M.Pharm full-time program. Must maintain minimum attendance and academic performance.
scholarships.apply_at: Through the admitting institution. AICTE disburses directly to student's bank account.
scholarships.deadline: Automatic upon admission with valid GATE/GPAT score.

scholarships.name: Uttarakhand Girl Child Incentive Scheme (Nanda Gaura Devi Kanya Dhan Yojana)
scholarships.category: Girl students from BPL families in Uttarakhand who pass 12th board exam
scholarships.amount: INR 51,000 as one-time grant after passing Class 12. Some revisions may increase amount.
scholarships.eligibility: Girl student of Uttarakhand domicile. From BPL (Below Poverty Line) family. Passed 12th board exam from Uttarakhand Board. SC/ST girls have additional benefits. Must be unmarried.
scholarships.apply_at: Through school/college or District Social Welfare Office. Also available on Uttarakhand e-District portal.
scholarships.deadline: Within 6 months of passing 12th board exam.

scholarships.name: HDFC Bank Parivartan ECS Scholarship
scholarships.category: Students from underprivileged backgrounds pursuing school education (Class 1-12), UG, PG, diploma, and professional courses
scholarships.amount: Up to INR 75,000/year for school students. Up to INR 1,20,000/year for UG/PG students. Up to INR 1,50,000/year for professional course students.
scholarships.eligibility: Indian citizen. Family income below INR 8,00,000/year. Scored 55%+ in previous exam (50% for Class 6-12). Not receiving any other scholarship. Preference to women, differently abled, and marginalized communities.

scholarships.name: MCM Scholarship (Merit cum Means Scholarship - UGC)
scholarships.category: Meritorious students from economically weaker sections pursuing professional & technical courses at UGC-recognized universities
scholarships.amount: Full tuition fee reimbursement + INR 1,000/month maintenance (hostellers: INR 2,000/month). Maximum INR 2,00,000/year.
scholarships.eligibility: Admitted to UGC-recognized university in professional course. Family income below INR 4,50,000/year. Must have scored 50%+ in qualifying exam. Admission through merit-based selection.
scholarships.apply_at: Through university/college. UGC allocates slots to universities.
